merges.merged_rewrite_direction: Give Chapter 10 an early orienting beat before Yor's domestic material takes over: confirm that Damian was moved at dawn, the WS-7 lead is now driving Anya's surveillance direction, and whether the report was delayed or rerouted. The reader needs to learn what happened at first light so the safe-house climax converts into sustained pressure rather than reading as a skipped chapter.
merges.reason: Both findings target the Ch9-to-Ch10 handoff and diagnose the same problem: the consequence chain from the safe house is silently stepped over. LW05-001 is more specific about the emotional and operational threads being shelved; LW04-001 adds the WS-7 surveillance-post detail. Merged direction captures both.

merges.merged_rewrite_direction: Dramatize a portion of the 0600 Falk watch on-page before the gala material: even a short beat of the desk going blind and Anya realizing the useful moment has passed would preserve momentum. Let the null result force the Kreuzfeld salvage decision in real time rather than reporting it in retrospective summary. If full dramatization would unbalance the chapter, compress to a scene-fragment: the desk, the silence, the decision to go north.
merges.reason: Both findings target Ch12 and diagnose the same problem: Ch11 loads the 0600 watch as the next live test, then Ch12 reports the result in retrospective summary. LW06-002 includes the fallback option of a compressed beat; LW05-003 adds the alternative of lowering Ch11's terminal promise. Merged direction prioritizes the dramatization within Ch12's scope.

merges.merged_rewrite_direction: Bridge the Loid-reckoning handoff at the opening of Chapter 16. Either give a short on-page home beat with Loid before Anya goes to Damian's apartment, or establish on the first page that Loid deliberately postponed the conversation overnight with a visible reason. The reader must see the deferral decision, not infer it.
merges.reason: Both findings target Ch16 and diagnose the identical problem: Ch15 ends on Loid's coldest demand ('Not in pieces. All of it.'), then Ch16 skips the handoff. Same counterpart excerpt (L8813), nearly identical rewrite directions. LW07-002 offers more specific options for the bridge.

canonical_choices.value: Damian's memorabilia box contains: a spelling certificate, one dry pen that doesn't work, an athletics ribbon, a festival program, and the Eden Academy class photo. These are the canonical contents.
canonical_choices.grounding: First establishment in Ch7 L4291-4319. The continuity sheet confirms the class photo but does not enumerate other items; Ch7 is the first on-page inventory and therefore authoritative for surface facts.

canonical_choices.value: Damian is in the third row, second from the right in the Eden Academy class photo.
canonical_choices.grounding: First establishment in Ch7 L4299. Ch16 L9048 contradicts with 'front row.' Since the box is presented as containing one class photo and Ch7 establishes the position first, Ch7 is authoritative.

canonical_choices.value: Four hostiles neutralized on the south terrace, not three.
canonical_choices.grounding: Ch15 dramatizes the action (L8591) and Yuri's post-action report confirms 'Four hostiles down' (L8645). Cross-chapter audit (grep-verified) is most authoritative on factual counts. Ch17 L10229 must change 'three men' to 'four.'

canonical_choices.value: The 'three months ago' at Ch17 L10191 refers to Anya's birthday (late December or January), not the gala (late October). The text must make this referent explicit.
canonical_choices.grounding: Timeline establishes the gala in late October and Ch17 in late March. Late October to late March is approximately five months, making the gala arithmetically wrong for 'three months.' A birthday in late December/January places the referent at three months before late March, which is correct. The thought is triggered by the word 'birthday,' confirming this reading. Damian already knew her name by then (confirmed at the gala two months prior).

consistency_directives.rule: Audit every revised passage for hedge words ('something,' 'somehow,' 'seemed to,' 'a kind of,' 'almost'). Replace with specifics wherever the referent can be identified. Intentional vagueness is permitted only when a character is genuinely uncertain or a telepathic signal is genuinely fragmentary. The default must be precision, not approximation.
consistency_directives.reason: Eight of seventeen chapters exceed the 1-per-800-words threshold for 'something' alone, and the remaining chapters were not audited with the same density metric. The drafting model defaults to hedge words as placeholder specificity; this tendency must be overridden in every chapter, not just the flagged ones.

consistency_directives.rule: In any revised dialogue exchange of three or more volleys, ensure at least one moment of rhythmic imbalance: a line that lands wrong, a response at the wrong register, a silence where speech was expected, or a sentence the speaker visibly regrets starting. Do not produce perfectly weighted stichomythia where every line advances the theme at the same pace and length.
consistency_directives.reason: Three flagged chapters show the same pattern: dialogue exchanges where every line is the perfect weight and length for its dramatic position, producing a composed-text feel rather than the disorder of human speech under pressure. This is a drafting-model tendency that would affect any dialogue-heavy scene, not just the flagged ones.

consistency_directives.rule: Once a chapter's dramatic climax has landed, close within 10-15 lines. Do not extend into narratorial meditation, moral processing, or enumeration of what the climax means. End on a physical detail, an unresolved image, or a single unprocessed sensation. The reader carries the meaning; the chapter's job is to leave them in the collision, not to summarize it.
consistency_directives.reason: Four chapters extend 25-55 lines past their natural dramatic close with extended meditation that restates the climax's significance. This is a drafting tendency toward wrapping emotional events in explanatory prose that would likely recur in any revised chapter ending.

context_injections.cross_chapter_context: This finding is routed to Ch2; Ch1's ending cityscape is outside Ch2's revision scope. Execute the second alternative: cut or replace the Berlint-at-dusk atmospheric framing from Ch2's opening (around L370) and enter the Ministry scene more directly. Do not attempt to modify Ch1's ending.

Ch7 L4291-4319 establishes Damian's box contents as: a spelling certificate, one dry pen that doesn't work, an athletics ribbon, a festival program, and the Eden Academy class photo. Ch13's re-inventory must match these items exactly. Any additional items in Ch13 should be framed as previously unnoticed rather than replacing prior items. See canonical_choice 'box_inventory' for the authoritative list.

This finding is routed to Ch3; Ch2's fragment stack is outside Ch3's revision scope. Execute the Ch3-specific fix: use the Academy reception or its aftermath to cash at least one key bureaucratic fragment (April fourteen, the forum list, or the south route) into plain-language stakes, so the reader enters Ch4 with a clearer sense of what the machinery is building toward. Do not attempt to strip Ch2's fragments.

pass_reassignments.reason: The finding targets recycled dialogue formulas ('not an answer,' 'accurate,' 'that's not what I asked') and character-specific speech patterns across chapters 3-5. This is an idiolect and dialogue cadence issue, not a sentence-level prose quality issue. P2 handles dialogue voice and character-specific speech; P3 handles prose rhythm, word choice, and copyedit-level quality.
